sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.1 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a finite non-abelian group, and$Z(G)$its centre. One can associate a graph$_G$with$G$as follows: take$G Z(G)$as vertices of$_G$and join two vertices$x$and$y$if$xy =yx$. Let$H$be a finite non-abelian group such that$_G _H$. [r]{b)} If$H$is nilpotent, is it true that$G$is nilpotent? \ \ Comment of 2009:\/ This is true if$|H|=|G|$(A.\,Abdollahi, S.\,Akbari, H.\,R.\,Maimani, J.~Algebra, 298 (...
